nodes.name: Start
nodes.notes:
═════════════════════════
  NODE 1: WORKFLOW TRIGGER (EMAIL WORKFLOW ENTRY POINT)  ═════════════════════════
  This is the entry point for the Email workflow.

  What data arrives here:
  When called from parent workflow, receives:

  - workflow_run_id (CRITICAL):
    - Parent workflow's execution ID
    - Example: 67890
    - Used to query email_queue table
    - Links to all jobs processed in parent workflow run
    - This is THE KEY that ties the entire
      system together

  - companies_processed (optional):
    - Summary count from parent workflow
    - Example: 40
    - For logging/debugging
    - Not used in email generation

  - query_timestamp (optional):
    - When parent workflow completed
    - Example: "2025-12-29T14:30:00.000Z"
    - For audit trail and debugging

  Data flow summary with example:

  Loop Companies starts → execution_id = 67890
  (becomes workflow_run_id)
    ↓
    ├─ Company 1: Anthropic
    │  └─ Loop Jobs called with
    │     _context_workflow_run_id = 67890
    │     ├─ Job 1: Senior PM → AI eval →
    │        email_queue (workflow_run_id: 67890)
    │     ├─ Job 2: Lead PM → AI eval →
    │        email_queue (workflow_run_id: 67890)
    │     └─ Job 8: Principal PM → AI eval →
    │        email_queue (workflow_run_id: 67890)
    │
    ├─ Company 2: OpenAI
    │  └─ Loop Jobs called with
    │     _context_workflow_run_id = 67890
    │     ├─ Job 1: Technical PM → AI eval →
    │        email_queue (workflow_run_id: 67890)
    │     └─ Job 5: Staff PM → AI eval →
    │        email_queue (workflow_run_id: 67890)
    │
    └─ All companies done (40 total)
       └─ Workflow Summary node:
          { workflow_run_id: 67890 }
          └─ Triggers Send Email workflow
             (THIS WORKFLOW)
             ↓
             Load Email Queue: WHERE
             workflow_run_id = 67890
             Result: All 13 jobs (8 from Anthropic
             + 5 from OpenAI)
             ↓
             Aggregate and Sort: Order by
             overall_score DESC
             ↓
             Build Email: Generate HTML with
             summary stats
             ↓
             Send Jobs Email: Gmail delivery →
             User inbox

  Critical note on workflow_run_id:

  The workflow_run_id is THE MASTER KEY that
  connects everything:

  1. CREATED BY: Loop Companies workflow (its
     $execution.id)
  2. PROPAGATED TO: Every job via
     _context_workflow_run_id
  3. STORED IN: email_queue table with every job card
  4. PASSED TO: This email workflow for querying
  5. RESULT: Single query retrieves all jobs
     from entire daily run

  Next node: Load Email Queue

nodes.name: Load Email Queue
nodes.notes:
═════════════════════════
  NODE 2: LOAD EMAIL QUEUE FROM DATABASE  ═════════════════════════
  Queries the email_queue table to retrieve all
  jobs processed in the current workflow run.

  What this node does:
  - Connects to n8n data table: "email_queue"
  - Filters by workflow_run_id from parent
    workflow (or manual trigger)
  - Returns ALL jobs across ALL companies from
    this workflow run
  - Each row is one complete job with HTML card
    and metadata

  Table schema (columns stored):

  1. workflow_run_id (number):
     - PRIMARY FILTER FIELD (indexed for
       performance)
     - Groups all jobs from single workflow run
     - Example: 67890
     - This is how we know which jobs belong together

  2. recommendation (string):
     - AI's recommendation category
     - Values: EXCELLENT_MATCH | GOOD_MATCH |
       CONSIDER | POOR_FIT | REJECT
     - Used for filtering and grouping in next node
     - Determines badge color in email

  3. overall_score (number):
     - AI's numeric score (1-10)
     - Primary sort field (higher = better match)
     - Used to order jobs in email (best first)

  4. html_card (string):
     - Complete, formatted HTML job card
     - Self-contained, ready to insert in email
     - Generated by Loop Jobs workflow's Format
       Job Card node
     - Size: ~2-5KB per job
     - Contains: title, company, salary,
       location, AI assessment, scores

  5. job_id (string):
     - Unique identifier from Apify
     - Links back to raw jobs_test_1 table
     - Used for deduplication and tracking

  6. company_name (string):
     - Company posting the job
     - Example: "Anthropic", "OpenAI", "Google"
     - Could be used for company-based grouping
       (not currently used)

  7. job_title (string):
     - Job title for reference
     - Example: "Senior Technical Product Manager"
     - Stored separately for quick reference
       without parsing HTML

  Next node receives:
  - Array of job objects
  - Each with html_card field
  - Each with scoring metadata
  - Ready for aggregation and sorting

  Next node: Aggregate and Sort Jobs

nodes.name: Aggregate and Sort Jobs
nodes.notes:
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  NODE: AGGREGATE AND SORT JOBS
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Reduces N job items → 1 aggregated summary item.

  Input:  N items from Load Email Queue
  Output: 1 item with category counts + sorted HTML

  Transformations:
    - Count jobs by category: excellent / strong /
      consider / poor
    - Sort all jobs by overall_score descending
    - Concatenate html_card fields with spacer divs

  See jsCode for full implementation.

  Next node: Build Email

nodes.name: Success Response
nodes.notes:
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  NODE: SUCCESS RESPONSE
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Builds a structured status object after email
  delivery and returns it to the parent workflow.

  Input:  SMTP send result + Build Email metadata
  Output: Unified status with email stats

  See jsCode for output schema.

nodes.name: Empty Response
nodes.notes:
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  NODE: EMPTY RESPONSE
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Returns a skip status when workflow_run_id is
  absent. Allows the parent workflow to continue
  without sending an email.

  Input:  Item from If node false branch
  Output: Structured skip status

  See jsCode for output schema.

nodes.name: If matches
nodes.notes:
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  NODE: IF — WORKFLOW_RUN_ID GUARD
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Purpose: Gate execution on a valid workflow_run_id.
  Prevents email generation when no run ID is available.

  Condition:
    workflow_run_id EXISTS (string, not null/empty)

  True  → output[0] → Aggregate and Sort Jobs
    workflow_run_id is present; proceed with email

  False → output[1] → Empty Response
    workflow_run_id is missing; return skip status
    to parent workflow

  When workflow_run_id may be absent:
    - Workflow triggered manually without input
    - Parent workflow failed before passing run ID
    - Development or test invocations

  alwaysOutputData: false
    Only one branch fires per execution.

  Next nodes:
    True  → Aggregate and Sort Jobs
    False → Empty Response

nodes.name: Build Match Email
nodes.notes:
════════════════════════════════════════════════
  NODE 4: BUILD EMAIL (HTML EMAIL GENERATOR)  ════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Purpose: Create professional, responsive HTML email with job digest
  Input: Single aggregated item with statistics and combined HTML
  Output: Complete email with dynamic subject line and formatted HTML body

  Key features:
  - Dynamic subject line based on job quality
  - Professional HTML email template
  - Responsive design for mobile/desktop
  - Color-coded statistics (green/blue/amber/gray)
  - Conditional content (highlight for excellent matches)
  - Email client compatible (inline styles)

  Next node: Send Jobs Email

nodes.name: Send Match Email
nodes.notes:
=═══════════════════════════════════
  NODE 5: SEND EMAIL (SMTP DELIVERY)
  Purpose: Deliver formatted HTML email digest
  to recipient
════════════════════════════════════

  SMTP Configuration:
  - From: "[Email Name <email@address>]"
  - To: Dynamic from Start node (see below)
  - Subject: {{ $json.subject }} (from Build Email)
  - Body: {{ $json.html_body }} (formatted HTML)

  CRITICAL: Recipient Address Uses [1] Index

  {{ $('Start').all()[1].json.full_name + ' <' +
     $('Start').all()[1].json.email + '>' }}

  Parent workflow passes TWO items to Start node:
  - Item [0]: Workflow summary (workflow_run_id,
    companies_processed)
  - Item [1]: User configuration (full_name,
    email, preferences)

  This node accesses Item [1] for recipient info.

parameters.jsCode:
// ==================================================================== // NODE: BUILD NOMATCH EMAIL // ==================================================================== // workflow_run_id comes from Start directly (Aggregate and Sort Jobs // did not run in this path). const workflowRunId = $('Start').first().json.workflow_run_id; const today = new Date(); const formattedDate = today.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { weekday: 'long', year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric' }); const subject = `📭 No new matches today — ${formattedDate}`; const emailHtml = ` Daily Job Digest

🎯 Your Daily Job Digest

${formattedDate}

📭

No new matches today

No job listings matched your criteria in the last 24 hours.
Check back tomorrow.

💡 Tip

If you're seeing few or no matches over several days, try adding more companies to your watchlist — a broader company list generally produces more results. You can also review your title search filters to make sure they aren't too narrow.

If you need help adjusting your configuration, contact your RoleFinder administrator.

📬 Automated Job Monitoring System

Workflow Run ID: #${workflowRunId}

`; return [{ json: { subject: subject, html_body: emailHtml, workflow_run_id: workflowRunId } }];
